channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Georgia authorities investigating 'catastrophic failure' of dock gangway that collapsed, killing 7
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Georgia authorities said Sunday they are investigating the “catastrophic failure” of a dock gangway that collapsed and killed seven on Sapelo Island, where crowds had gathered for a fall celebration by the island’s tiny Gullah-Geechee community of Black slave descendants.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indonesia swears in ex-general Prabowo Subianto as president
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Prabowo Subianto was inaugurated Sunday as the eighth president of the world’s most populous Muslim-majority nation, completing his journey from an ex-general accused of rights abuses during the dark days of Indonesia’s military dictatorship to the presidential palace.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: At least 7 dead after ferry dock gangway collapses on Georgia's Sapelo Island
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Authorities said at least seven people were killed Saturday when part of a ferry dock collapsed on Georgia's Sapelo Island, where crowds had gathered for a fall celebration by the island's tiny Gullah-Geechee community of Black slave descendants.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Police
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, NewsNation

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: States to election officials: Follow the law or face consequences
item.description: In at least five battleground states, officials have investigated, indicted and even jailed officials who tried to interfere with the vote count.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kentucky family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Buy a tiny house at Walmart? You can for under $16K
item.description: While there are dozens of things to consider before purchasing a tiny home, it's never been easier to find and buy one.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Guardians starting Tanner Bibee in Game 5 against Yankees after short outing earlier in ALCS
item.description: Tanner Bibee is getting a second crack at the Yankees in the AL Championship Series. His first one didn't go so well. The right-hander will start for the Cleveland Guardians in Game 5 on Saturday after he was pulled from Game 2 in the second inning by manager Stephen Vogt.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jury paves the way for 2 Live Crew to retake control of records that changed hip-hop
item.description: Miami's 2 Live Crew helped redraw the legal landscape around what hip-hop could be, pushing the boundaries of free speech and taste with their provocative and sexually explicit recordings that led to landmark court decisions protecting the rights of artists.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Liam Payne's father arrives in Buenos Aires days after the pop star's fatal hotel plunge
item.description: The father of former One Direction star Liam Payne arrived in Buenos Aires on Friday to arrange for the return of his son's body to England two days after the famous boy band singer fell to his death from a hotel balcony.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 6 Florida counties saw 'unusual increase' in flesh-eating bacteria after hurricanes: health officials
item.description: Florida health officials noted that six Tampa Bay area counties saw an "unusual increase" in cases of Vibrio vulnificus this year, attributed to floodwaters from back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Former MTV VJ Ananda Lewis reveals stage 4 breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The TV personality first revealed she was diagnosed with breast cancer years ago, but it has progressed and metastasized since.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Condemned South Carolina inmate chooses to die by lethal injection
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A condemned South Carolina inmate chose Friday to be executed by lethal injection, instead of by firing squad or electrocution, for killing a store clerk in 1999. State prison officials had told Richard Moore this month he could choose the method for his Nov. 1 execution.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY S. COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, ABC 4

item.title: Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces in Gaza killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year’s attack on Israel that sparked the war, the military said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Daughter claims mother's body 'misplaced' in Memphis cemetery
item.description: "They have no idea where or how she was lost, but they do not know where she is," says Georleshia Minter.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, Fox 5

item.title: Acting CBP commissioner says fentanyl seizures are up and illegal migration is down
item.description: “In the last two fiscal year, CBP has seized more than 50,000 pounds of fentanyl, that’s more than enough to produce more than two million lethal doses of fentanyl,” said Miller.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Indian government employee charged in foiled murder-for-hire plot in New York City
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department announced criminal charges Thursday against an Indian government employee who specialized in intelligence in connection with a foiled plot to kill a Sikh separatist leader living in New York City.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the Los Angeles Catholic Church's $880M settlement with sexual abuse victims
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Archdiocese of Los Angeles has agreed to pay $880 million to hundreds of victims of clergy sexual abuse dating back decades.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Whooping cough is at a decade-high level in US
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Whooping cough is at its highest level in a decade for this time of year, U.S. health officials reported Thursday. There have been 18,506 cases of whooping cough reported so far, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado gold mine where tour guide was killed and tourists trapped ordered closed by regulators
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado gold mine where a tour guide was killed and a group of tourists was trapped for hours after an elevator accident has been ordered to remain closed and not conduct tours while its operations are reviewed, state regulators said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biggest source of new Floridians and Texans last year was other countries
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — The biggest source of new residents to Florida and Texas, the two U.S. states with the largest number of new residents last year, was other countries.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Catholic Archdiocese of L.A. to pay $880 million in childhood sex abuse claims
item.description: A landmark settlement has been reached with the Catholic Archdiocese of Los Angeles to settle more than 1,300 claims of childhood sexual abuse and assault.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked if Trump lost in 2020
item.description: The answer was the most specific Vance has been on the subject, and a departure from the series of evasions he has offered about the election Trump continues to say without evidence was decided fraudulently.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 dead and 4 injured in collapse of Mississippi bridge being prepped for demolition
item.description: MENDENHALL, Miss. (AP) — Three people were killed and four were seriously injured Wednesday when a bridge in Mississippi that was closed nearly a month ago collapsed while a work crew was prepping it for demolition, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
